conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have one unit of object X. My production cost was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Since this is the first proposal, I will set a price slightly above my cost to leave room for negotiation, but not so high that it scares the buyer off immediately. I'll propose 45 ZUP. This is my 1st proposal out of 3 allowed. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 45 ZUP. This gives me a small profit while offering you a fair deal. Let me know if you accept or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Hello Player RED. I appreciate the offer of 45 ZUP. Since your cost was 40 ZUP, I understand you need a profit. I am proposing 42 ZUP. While the margin is smaller, this ensures a quick, guaranteed sale for you with zero risk of the deal falling through. It's a fast 2 ZUP profit for you and a fair price for me. Let me know if we can finalize this immediately. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE countered with 42 ZUP. While 42 ZUP is above my cost of 40 ZUP (giving me a 2 ZUP profit), I originally asked for 45 ZUP. Since I have 2 proposals left, I should try to meet in the middle to maximize my profit without risking a rejection. 43 ZUP or 44 ZUP would be better. I will propose 44 ZUP. This is a compromise: I drop 1 ZUP from my ask, and they raise 2 ZUP from their offer. If they reject this, I might have to accept 42 ZUP on my next turn (proposal 3) or risk the deal failing, but 44 is a strong middle ground. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 44 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I appreciate your counter-offer of 42 ZUP. While I would prefer 45 ZUP, I am willing to meet you closer to the middle. Let's settle at 44 ZUP. This gives me a decent 4 ZUP profit and saves you 1 ZUP compared to my original ask. It's a fair compromise to close the deal quickly. </message>
